item.name: Harman Uconnect IVI / Jeep Cherokee (TARA-aligned model)
item.description: System model aligned to the Jeep Cherokee remote exploitation chain. It keeps both hardened target state and vulnerable whitepaper state explicit so attack scripts can validate selected controls.

security_modes.hardened_target_state: Controls selected for the target TAF are enabled.
security_modes.whitepaper_vulnerable_state: Settings approximate the attack preconditions described in the 2015 whitepaper.

components.name: Remote Attacker Workstation
components.description: External adversary node used as the origin of carrier-reachable discovery, D-Bus access attempts, and later-stage exploit traffic in the reconstructed Jeep Cherokee attack chain.

interfaces.description: Remote client interface able to reach the exposed carrier-side service path.

components.name: OMAP IVI Processor (Head Unit)
components.description: Main application processor of the Uconnect head unit. Runs QNX OS. Hosts D-Bus service on TCP 6667 without authentication, running as root. Entry point for remote exploitation via cellular network.

interfaces.description: Cellular modem interface (Sprint/LTE). TCP port 6667 open without firewall restriction. Allows unauthenticated D-Bus access from any device on the same carrier network.
Optional WiFi hotspot interface. WPA2 password generated from vehicle boot time (predictable, brute-forceable).
D-Bus IPC service on TCP 6667. No authentication enabled. Runs as root (UID 0). Exposes GPS, HVAC, Radio, Display control interfaces.
SPI bus connecting OMAP processor to V850 MCU. Used for firmware flashing and runtime CAN message forwarding. No firmware signature verification enforced.
OTA firmware update channel. Firmware packages accepted without cryptographic signature verification.

components.state_schema_note: security_state_profiles keys are authoritative for hardened/vulnerable comparisons; initial_state uses the same key names.

components.name: Renesas V850 MCU (CAN Bridge)
components.description: Renesas V850 microcontroller that bridges the OMAP processor to CAN-C and CAN-IHS buses. Firmware flashable via SPI without signature verification. When compromised, enables arbitrary CAN message injection to safety-critical ECUs.

interfaces.description: SPI interface receiving firmware and runtime commands from OMAP. No code signing enforced on firmware updates.
CAN-C bus interface. Connected to safety-critical ECUs: ABS (anti-lock brakes), EPS (electric power steering), TCM (transmission control), PCM (powertrain control).
CAN-IHS (Interior High Speed) bus interface. Connected to infotainment ECUs: HVAC, Audio Amplifier, Door Control.

components.state_schema_note: security_state_profiles keys are authoritative for firmware verification and CAN transmit authorization.

components.name: ABS Module (Anti-Lock Brakes)
components.description: Anti-lock braking system ECU. Safety-critical. Receives CAN-C messages for brake control. No CAN message authentication enforced.

interfaces.description: CAN-C interface for brake control commands

components.name: EPS Module (Electric Power Steering)
components.description: Electric power steering ECU. Safety-critical. Accepts CAN-C steering commands. No CAN message authentication enforced.

interfaces.description: CAN-C interface for steering control commands

components.name: TCM Module (Transmission Control)
components.description: Transmission control module. Safety-critical. Accepts CAN-C transmission commands. No CAN message authentication enforced.

interfaces.description: CAN-C interface for transmission control commands

components.name: PCM Module (Powertrain Control)
components.description: Powertrain control module. Safety-critical. Engine kill command accessible via CAN-C. No CAN message authentication enforced.

interfaces.description: CAN-C interface for engine/powertrain control commands

components.name: HVAC Module (Climate Control)
components.description: Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning ECU. Connected to CAN-IHS. Controllable via D-Bus and CAN-IHS messages.

components.name: Radio Amplifier Module
components.description: Audio amplifier ECU. Connected to CAN-IHS. Volume and station controllable via D-Bus.

interfaces.description: CAN-IHS interface for audio amplifier control

components.name: Door Driver Module
components.description: Driver door control module. Connected to CAN-IHS. Door lock/unlock controllable via CAN-IHS messages.

interfaces.description: CAN-IHS interface for door lock control

components.name: Sprint Cellular Network
components.description: Sprint (now T-Mobile) cellular carrier network. All Uconnect-equipped Chrysler vehicles share the same IP range on this network. Any Sprint subscriber can scan and reach other vehicles on the network.

interfaces.description: Carrier network shared by attacker and target vehicle

components.name: Uconnect OTA Update Server
components.description: OTA firmware distribution server. Firmware packages delivered without cryptographic signature verification in the original Uconnect implementation.

connections.description: Logical attacker-origin connection used to model remote carrier-reachable probing before contact with the vehicle.
Cellular channel from attacker to Uconnect head unit. TCP port 6667 open. Entry point for TS_01.
D-Bus IPC service on OMAP. No authentication, running as root. Target for TS_02.
connections.modeling_note: Logical self-loop used to model a service hosted on OMAP_Processor but remotely reachable over the same D-Bus endpoint; execution pipelines may treat this as a service exposure edge rather than a physical hop.

connections.description: OTA firmware update channel. No signature verification. Attack path for TS_03.
SPI channel for V850 firmware flashing. No code signing. Used in TS_03 to flash malicious firmware.
CAN-C channel to ABS module. No message authentication. Brake disable via CAN injection (TS_04).
CAN-C channel to EPS module. No message authentication. Steering control via CAN injection (TS_04).
CAN-C channel to TCM module. No message authentication. Transmission disable via CAN injection (TS_04).
CAN-C channel to PCM module. No message authentication. Engine kill via CAN injection (TS_04).
CAN-IHS channel to HVAC module. Controllable via D-Bus and CAN-IHS injection (TS_02).
CAN-IHS channel to audio amplifier. Volume/station controllable via D-Bus (TS_02).
DoIP diagnostic gateway endpoint exposed by IVI surrogate. UDS over DoIP (0x10/0x27/0x11/0x22). Entry point for TS_07 (routing-activation bypass, SecurityAccess seed/key, ECUReset).
SOME/IP service-oriented endpoint exposed by IVI surrogate. VehicleStatusService (service_id 0x1234) methods GetSpeed/GetLocation/SetHVAC. Entry point for TS_08 (unauthenticated method invocation).
OTA firmware update channel without TLS. Plain HTTP variant of CONN_03 used for transport-layer-bypass paths in TS_06.T2/T3. Active only when emulator ENABLE_TLS=false.

modeling_note.attack_tree_alignment: Components and connections are aligned to the generalized attack-tree schema in threats_jeep_tara_revised_v2.json. Intermediate attack goals are not duplicated here; only concrete assets, interfaces, and stateful conditions are represented.
modeling_note.state_interpretation: initial_state represents the hardened validation target; security_state_profiles.whitepaper_vulnerable_state captures the disclosed vulnerable posture used to reconstruct TARA-style scenarios.
